paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah said unto Moses, See, I have made thee as God to Pharaoh; and Aaron thy brother shall be thy prophet.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou shalt speak all that I command thee; and Aaron thy brother shall speak unto Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go out of his land.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And I will harden Pharaoh`s heart, and multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But Pharaoh will not hearken unto you, and I will lay my hand upon Egypt, and bring forth my hosts, my people the children of Israel, out of the land of Egypt by great judgments.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the Egyptians shall know that I am Jehovah, when I stretch forth my hand upon Egypt, and bring out the children of Israel from among them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses and Aaron did so; as Jehovah commanded them, so did they.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses was fourscore years old, and Aaron fourscore and three years old, when they spake unto Pharaoh.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: When Pharaoh shall speak unto you, saying, Show a wonder for you; then thou shalt say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and cast it down before Pharaoh, that it become a serpent.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses and Aaron went in unto Pharaoh, and they did so, as Jehovah had commanded: and Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh and before his servants, and it became a serpent.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then Pharaoh also called for the wise men and the sorcerers: and they also, the magicians of Egypt, did in like manner with their enchantments.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: For they cast down every man his rod, and they became serpents: but Aaron`s rod swallowed up their rods.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Pharaoh`s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as Jehovah had spoken.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah said unto Moses, Pharaoh`s heart is stubborn, he refuseth to let the people go.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Get thee unto Pharaoh in the morning; lo, he goeth out unto the water; and thou shalt stand by the river`s brink to meet him; and the rod which was turned to a serpent shalt thou take in thy hand.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And thou shalt say unto him, Jehovah, the God of the Hebrews, hath sent me unto thee, saying, Let my people go, that they may serve me in the wilderness: and, behold, hitherto thou hast not hearkened.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thus saith Jehovah, In this thou shalt know that I am Jehovah: behold, I will smite with the rod that is in my hand upon the waters which are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the fish that are in the river shall die, and the river shall become foul; and the Egyptians shall loathe to drink water from the river.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah said unto Moses, Say unto Aaron, Take thy rod, and stretch out thy hand over the waters of Egypt, over their rivers, over their streams, and over their pools, and over all their ponds of water, that they may become blood; and there shall be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, both in vessels of wood and in vessels of stone.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses and Aaron did so, as Jehovah commanded; and he lifted up the rod, and smote the waters that were in the river, in the sight of Pharaoh, and in the sight of his servants; and all the waters that were in the river were turned to blood.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the fish that were in the river died; and the river became foul, and the Egyptians could not drink water from the river; and the blood was throughout all the land of Egypt.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the magicians of Egypt did in like manner with their enchantments: and Pharaoh`s heart was hardened, and he hearkened not unto them; as Jehovah had spoken.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Pharaoh turned and went into his house, neither did he lay even this to heart.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And all the Egyptians digged round about the river for water to drink; for they could not drink of the water of the river.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And seven days were fulfilled, after that Jehovah had smitten the river.
